senses.definition: to slice something before the normal or intended time
examples: They plan to preslice the vegetables for the stir-fry.
Preslice the meat to speed up the cooking process.

senses.definition: already sliced before use
examples: Presliced bread is convenient for quick meals.
We bought presliced apples for the fruit platter.

senses.definition: instances of cutting something into slices before normal use (plural of preslice)
examples: The preslices were ready for the sandwich setup.
We tasted a few preslices to check thickness.

senses.definition: the act of slicing something before the usual time or preparation
examples: Preslicing the vegetables saves time during dinner.
Preslicing is common in meal prep.

senses.definition: to smooth something before a later step or process
examples: Presmooth the surface before applying the coating.
Presmooth the edges to avoid roughness.

senses.definition: to soak something beforehand, especially to soften or clean
examples: Presoak the beans overnight.
Presoak the grains to reduce cooking time.

senses.definition: soaked beforehand
examples: Presoaked beans cook faster.
Presoaked oats soften quickly in hot water.

senses.definition: the act of soaking something beforehand
examples: Presoaking legumes saves time later.
Presoaking grains is recommended for some recipes.

senses.definition: instances of soaking beforehand (plural of presoaks)
examples: The presoaks helped reduce cooking time.
Note the presoaks before adding to the pot.

senses.definition: before or relating to social behavior, not yet socialized
examples: Presocial insects have not formed complex colonies.
Some presocial experiments study early social behavior.

senses.definition: an early or theoretical state before socialism
examples: Presocialism is discussed in some political histories.
The term presocialism appears in debates about economic development.

senses.definition: a supporter or advocate of presocialism
examples: The presocialist argued for early collective ownership.
Presocialists influenced early political debates.

senses.definition: before the formation of a star; relating to the earliest solar system material
examples: Presolar grains are found in meteorites.
Presolar isotopes provide clues about stellar processes.

senses.definition: sold before being produced or delivered; pre-sold
examples: The book was presold to fans before publication.
Presold tickets were cheaper.

senses.definition: to request or invite in advance, often to prepare for a larger solicitation
examples: The company may presolicit bids for the contract.
Presolicit feedback helps shape the proposal.

senses.definition: an advance request or invitation for proposals or bids
examples: The presolicitation announced a new procurement process.
Respondents reviewed the presolicitation before submitting bids.

senses.definition: a proposed or pre-existing solution considered before final decision
examples: The team discussed a presolution to the issue.
A presolution was put forward during the meeting.

senses.definition: solvated before a final chemical state is reached; having been treated with solvent in advance
examples: Presolvated compounds were prepared for reaction.
The presolvated solution showed different conductivity.

senses.definition: to solve something before a larger process or to determine answers early
examples: The algorithm can presolve simple cases.
Presolve the issue before filing the report.

senses.definition: already solved or resolved before a later step
examples: Presolved questions simplified the discussion.
Presolved issues were moved to the next phase.

senses.definition: solves something in advance or resolves beforehand
examples: The system presolves simple conflicts automatically.
They presolves routine issues to keep things moving.

senses.definition: the act of solving something before a larger process or stage
examples: Presolving is used to streamline workflows.
Presolving minor issues helps avoid delays.

senses.definition: a lyric or song that is prepared in advance, possibly an introductory track
examples: The presong played before the main act.
They released a presong to promote the album.

senses.definition: a person or stage before the sophomore year; a pre-sophomore
examples: The presophomore group prepared for the second year.
In the university, presophomore activities began early.

senses.definition: to sort items before another process, such as packing or shipping
examples: Presort the mail before loading onto the truck.
We presort products to improve warehouse efficiency.
